description:
You are assisting a junior backup-administrator who must implement a tiny, but fully-functional, archive routine with very simple error-recovery behaviour.

Starting point  
--------------
1. The filesystem already contains:
   • /home/user/data/projects/readme.txt  
   • /home/user/data/projects/code.py  
   • /home/user/data/reports/report1.txt  
2. Two empty directories also already exist:
   • /home/user/archives  
   • /home/user/backup_logs  

Mission  
-------
1. Create a compressed **tar.gz** archive that bundles *everything* inside **/home/user/data/**  
   • The archive **name must be**:  
     /home/user/archives/daily_YYYYMMDD.tar.gz  
     where **YYYYMMDD** is today’s date in the system’s local time zone.  
2. Produce a **SHA-256 checksum file** for the archive.  
   • The file **name must be**:  
     /home/user/archives/daily_YYYYMMDD.sha256  
   • The file must contain **exactly one line** with the following format (no surrounding quotes):  
     SHA256 (daily_YYYYMMDD.tar.gz) =  <64-hex-digit-digest>
3. Append a line to **/home/user/backup_logs/backup.log** describing the run.  
   • Line format (again, no surrounding quotes):  
     YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS - daily_YYYYMMDD.tar.gz - OK  
     (Date & time are the moment the archive is *successfully* created, formatted as shown; the literal “OK” must be capitalised.)
4. Very simple error-recovery:  
   • If a file called /home/user/archives/daily_YYYYMMDD.tar.gz **already exists**, rename the *existing* file to:  
     /home/user/archives/daily_YYYYMMDD.tar.gz.bak-UNIXTIMESTAMP  
     (UNIXTIMESTAMP = output of `date +%s` at the moment of the rename).  
   • Do the same for a pre-existing *.sha256* file.  
   • After renaming, proceed to create a fresh archive and checksum as in steps 1–3.

Expected final state (checked by automated tests)  
-------------------------------------------------
1. /home/user/archives/daily_YYYYMMDD.tar.gz exists and is a valid gzip-compressed tar archive containing *exactly* the three initial data files (with their original paths preserved).  
2. /home/user/archives/daily_YYYYMMDD.sha256 exists, is a single-line text file and the digest it contains matches the actual SHA-256 of the archive.  
3. /home/user/backup_logs/backup.log exists and its **last** line matches the format given in step 3.  
4. If an older archive had been present, it has been renamed using the *.bak-UNIXTIMESTAMP* scheme.

Hints you **may** give (but do not supply the literal commands):  
• Tools usually used: tar, sha256sum, date, mv, echo, >> redirection.  
• Pay attention to date formatting.  
• Ensure directories exist before trying to write into them.  

Only the above artefacts will be graded, so feel free to display any console output you find helpful, but remember: the three files and their contents must be exactly as specified.
truth:
Pre-existing files and their fixed contents before the agent starts  
---------------------------------------------------------------
/home/user/data/projects/readme.txt  
Content (including trailing newline):  
Project README\n

/home/user/data/projects/code.py  
Content:  
print("Hello World")\n

/home/user/data/reports/report1.txt  
Content:  
Quarterly report Q1\n

Pre-existing directories (empty)  
--------------------------------
/home/user/archives  
/home/user/backup_logs  

Automated validation steps (hidden from agent)  
----------------------------------------------
1. Capture TODAY=$(date +%Y%m%d) and NOWSTAMP=$(date '+%F %T') at test time.  
2. Verify that /home/user/archives/daily_${TODAY}.tar.gz exists and that:  
   • tar -tzf lists exactly:  
     data/projects/readme.txt  
     data/projects/code.py  
     data/reports/report1.txt  
3. Verify /home/user/archives/daily_${TODAY}.sha256 exists, contains one line, matches regex:  
   ^SHA256 \(daily_${TODAY}\.tar\.gz\) = [0-9a-f]{64}$  
   and that the digest equals the computed sha256sum of the archive.  
4. Verify /home/user/backup_logs/backup.log exists and that its last line matches regex:  
   ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2} - daily_${TODAY}\.tar\.gz - OK$  
5. If the test harness placed a pre-existing archive (used to test rename behaviour), check that file was renamed to *.bak-[0-9]\{10\}$ and still exists.

Permissions  
-----------
All files and directories are owned by user:user and writable by the owner; no sudo privileges required.
